title: Importing a Type Library
text: Importing a Type Library To simplify communication with external objects, you can import type library information using the command line switch /sti with /mwr =com . You can import a type library either by entering the PathToTypeLibrary , or by using the /pid subswitch with the logical name (ProgId) that maps to the type library. For example, enter a command according to the following syntax in the command line: /sti { /verbose } /mwr=com PathToTypeLibrary or /sti { /verbose } /mwr=com /pid ProgId { : Prefix } where: /verbose —produces a detailed description of the imported data. By default, a brief description is shown. Optional. PathToTypeLibrary —path to a type library. /pid —indicates that ProgId is a logical name that maps to a type library, for example word.document : Prefix —causes the prefix name to precede the signature names for each type definition found in the type library. Related Links /sti Import interface definitions to integrate call-out to web services, various types of middleware, or to generate C call-in stub code for all types of components.
